notes: Two kiro-cli lifetimes back-to-back, one per recordable session-end variant: (1) clean /quit after a no-op turn, (2) SIGKILL while idle in ready. Each variant asserts a ready→working→ready arc then process_exited and disappearance. Each variant's process_exited anchors the next variant's birth. Process exit deletes the session by design (kiro-cli matched by ExactName + cwd; pgrep stops matching the dead parent). The original 3rd variant (SIGKILL mid-turn while working) was dropped: kiro writes its transcript ATOMICALLY at turn-end, so a running turn is never observably working and a mid-turn kill leaves an empty transcript with no session row (see metadata caveats). Observations: kiro-cli JSONL carries no model/token/cost (FINDINGS.md §4), so model is 'unknown' and cost/tokens are zero.

text: Variant 1: first user prompt drives ready → working
Variant 1: turn completes back to ready
Variant 1: clean /quit — process_exited fires AFTER state returned to ready (the v1_turn_done → v1_exit chain proves 'State is ready at exit')
Variant 2: new session appears in ready after variant 1 finishes
Variant 2: user prompt drives ready → working
Variant 2: turn completes back to ready (idle window begins)

Variant 2: SIGKILL mid-idle — process_exited fires while session is in ready; no transition to working or waiting between v2_turn_done and process_exited (satisfies 'state never entered working post-exit')
